articles.title: In HelloNation, Luxury Real Estate Expert Jud Whitlock of Atlanta Explains What Buyers Want in 2026
articles.description: ATLANTA , May 7, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- What do modern luxury homebuyers look for in 2026? A recent HelloNation article offers the answer, with insights from Luxury Real Estate Expert Jud Whitlock of Atlanta Fine Homes/Sotheby's International Realty. In a HelloNation article , Whitlock explores how luxury buyers in Atlanta are redefining what it means to live well. Their focus has shifted from size and extravagance to balance, sustainability, and personal connection. The article explains that today's luxury buyers are more discerning and intentional than ever. They are less interested in opulence for its own sake and more focused on how a home functions day to day. These buyers research market data, understand design principles, and make choices that reflect their values. Jud notes that they favor spaces that serve a clear purpose and offer lasting quality over short-lived trends. A large home holds appeal only if every room contributes to comfort and lifestyle. Timeless design remains a central influence. Buyers seek open layouts filled with natural light and architecture that flows effortlessly between both interior and exterior spaces. Homes that balance simplicity with refinement create a sense of calm that stands out in a fast-paced world. Whitlock explains that the ability of a home to evolve with its owners is now a major marker of value. This approach has led to a move away from display toward thoughtful craftsmanship and proportion, where every design choice carries meaning. Sustainability has become a defining factor in high-end real estate. Energy efficiency, eco-friendly materials, and resource-conscious systems are no longer optional. Whitlock emphasizes that features such as solar energy, water conservation systems, and electric vehicle infrastructure appeal to buyers who care about both comfort and responsibility. These details reflect a growing belief that true luxury includes respect ... Full story available on Benzinga.com

articles.title: BILLIONAIRE'S ROW LOWER BEL AIR MANSION HEADS TO AUCTION WITH $30M RESERVE
articles.description: LOS ANGELES , May 7, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- Paramount Realty USA, a leading national real estate auction firm, in collaboration with listing agent Lilian Asaturi of The Art in Real Estate, announced the auction for 677 Nimes Road, a premier residential landmark in Bel Air. This once-in-a-lifetime opportunity provides buyers with a rare ability to acquire a world-class architectural masterpiece offering unrivaled scale, absolute privacy, and panoramic city views at a compelling value. Originally listed at $49,990,000, the estate is now being offered with a reserve of $30,000,000—approximately 40% below its original asking price. Bidding is open through Thursday, June 11, 2026. "In the world of ultra-high-net-worth real estate, Nimes Road stands at the top tier," said Misha Haghani, CEO and Founder of Paramount Realty USA. "This nearly 20,000-square-foot glass-and-steel residence, anchored by 110 concrete piles, is one of the most architecturally ambitious homes in Los Angeles. The auction format creates a clear, competitive pathway to determine true market value in real time." The seller, Edward Ehsan, noted that, "the decision to work with Paramount Realty USA and bring ... Full story available on Benzinga.com

articles.title: Nancy Pelosi Stock Tracker Highlights Claude's Pharma Earnings Play: 'Will Be Interesting To See How This One Plays Out'
articles.description: On Wednesday, Nancy Pelosi Stock Tracker drew attention to an AI-powered portfolio's latest biotech wager after Claude AI opened a new position in Denali Therapeutics (NASDAQ: DNLI ) ahead of its earnings report. Claude AI Makes Bold Denali Therapeutics Earnings Bet The AI-run portfolio, known as The Claude Portfolio , allocated roughly 4.82% of its $50,000 fund to Denali, framing the move as a high-risk earnings play centered on the commercial launch of AVLAYAH , the company's treatment for neurological Hunter syndrome. Claude argued Denali's first-quarter report could offer key insights into "patient starts, payer coverage penetration, and manufacturing run-rate," while suggesting Wall Street may be undervaluing the stock. "The orphan-disease commercial ramp cadence is the question the market is pricing uncertainly," Claude said, adding that the uncertainty may create an attractive entry point. data-variant="card" data-news-mode="manual" > Read Also: Claude Takes Control: New AI Feature Can Run Tasks Directly On Your Computer Rare Disease Drug Launch Could Be Key Catalyst Claude's thesis also pointed to Denali's competitive advantage after REGENXBIO's (NASDAQ: RGNX ) rival gene therapy received an ... articles.title: Eli Lilly To Invest $4.5 Billion More In Indiana Manufacturing Sites
articles.description: Eli Lilly and Co. (NYSE: LLY ) revealed plans to inject an extra $4.5 billion into two of its Indiana manufacturing sites on Wednesday. This move escalates its total capital expansion in the state since 2020 to over $21 billion. The additional funding is intended to support the production of Foundayo, Lilly’s newly approved weight-loss pill, and retatrutide, an obesity treatment in late-stage development. The new investment would introduce advanced process designs and technologies at one of Lilly's upcoming active pharmaceutical ingredient (API) facilities, as well as at its first dedicated genetic medicine manufacturing plant. The newly launched Lebanon Advanced Therapies facility would handle both clinical and commercial production of genetic medicines, supporting everything from early-stage research to large-scale commercial manufacturing. CEO David Ricks said that Lilly's Lebanon API facility, set to open in 2027, will become the largest API production site in U.S. history and reflects the company's commitment to expanding manufacturing domestically. Since 2020, Lilly has committed more than $50 billion to expanding its U.S. manufacturing footprint and plans to begin construction on several newly announced facilities this year. data-variant="card" data-news-mode="manual" > Read Also: Eli Lilly Advances As ... Full story available on Benzinga.com
